sourceMovie.title: Ad Astra
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ad Astra (2019) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James Gray's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Interstellar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ad Astra clicked for you, Interstellar (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ad Astra?
faq.answer: Try Interstellar, The Martian, Gravity—each shares DNA with Ad Astra while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ad Astra?
faq.answer: Ad Astra (2019) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ad Astra worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
